_comment: Example context object passed into a segment prompt during Evening block.
_note: All news items have been editorially transformed. Raw API data never appears here.

news.headline: Fed hikes rates again
news.radio_copy: The Fed just moved again — another twenty-five basis point hike. Inflation apparently hasn't gotten the memo. Your mortgage just got a little more painful.
talking_points: Has the Fed gone too far?
What does this mean for borrowing costs?

news.headline: EU advances AI liability bill
news.radio_copy: Brussels is moving fast on AI. Parliament voted to advance a bill that would make AI companies legally responsible for harm their models cause. Still developing — but if it passes, every major tech company operating in Europe is looking at a fundamentally different risk environment.
talking_points: How 'harm' gets defined is the whole fight

news.headline: Social platform whistleblower alleges algorithm boosted divisive content
news.radio_copy: A whistleblower inside one of the major platforms dropped a document today alleging the engagement algorithm was deliberately tuned for divisive content — even after internal research flagged the harm. Document is being verified. If it holds, this is the story of the week.

news.headline: Streaming wars heat up with surprise acquisition
news.radio_copy: One of the mid-tier streamers just got swallowed up. Consolidation in streaming is happening faster than most analysts predicted and the content library implications are genuinely interesting.

crypto._note: SOL up 12.4% — CRYPTO_SURGE trigger checked but did not fire (threshold is 10%, SOL qualifies but not BTC/ETH)
